sanskrit:
श्रीभगवानुवाच।
मय्यावेश्य मनो ये मां नित्ययुक्ता उपासते।
श्रद्धया परयोपेतास्ते मे युक्ततमा मताः॥
transliteration:
śrī-bhagavān uvāca
mayy āveśya mano ye māṁ nitya-yuktā upāsate
śraddhayā parayopetās te me yuktatamā matāḥ
literal_meaning: The Lord said: Those who fix the mind on Me, ever united, worship Me with transcendental faith—those I deem the most united.
contextual_meaning: Krishna operationalizes ‘perfection’ as sustained, exclusive attention on a single internalized object of devotion, maintained by disciplined repetition and infused with trust that transcends evidence.
psychological_state: Flow-like absorption in a personally meaningful archetype; ego boundaries soften while executive control remains, producing stable euthymia.
trigger_scenario: A burned-out software engineer begins each day with ten minutes of visualizing her deceased mentor’s smiling face and silently repeating his mantra; the rest of the day she codes, eats, and chats with colleagues while subtly holding that image in peripheral awareness, noticing stress spikes drop and motivation rise.
anchor_text: Keep your mind steadily on Me with trusting love.
